cashback.category: Grocery, departmental store & utility bill payments

loungeAccess.conditions: Railway lounge access only, not airport lounge access. 8 complimentary visits per year total, shared with add-on cardholders.

faqTemplateAnswers.Is this card lifetime free?: No. It has a joining and annual fee of ₹199 + taxes, though the joining fee is waived on ₹10,000 spend within 90 days and the annual fee is waived on ₹40,000 spend in the previous year.
faqTemplateAnswers.Is it worth it?: For a low, easily waivable ₹199 fee, the 2%/1% cashback on everyday grocery, utility and retail spends plus railway lounge access make it a reasonable low-maintenance cashback card, though the ₹50/month cashback caps limit upside for bigger spenders.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should get this card?: Everyday spenders on groceries, utility bills and retail who also take frequent train journeys and want a low-fee cashback card.
faqTemplateAnswers.Does it support UPI?: AU Bank cards can generally be linked to UPI apps; confirm current UPI support for this specific Visa variant directly with AU Bank.
faqTemplateAnswers.Can I get lounge access with this card?: No airport lounge access; the card offers 8 complimentary railway lounge visits per year, shared with add-on cardholders.
faqTemplateAnswers.What income is required to qualify?: AU Bank does not publish a fixed minimum income threshold; eligibility is assessed on income, credit history and existing relationship with the bank.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should avoid this card?: Big spenders who will regularly exceed the ₹50/month cashback caps, or frequent flyers wanting airport lounge access, should look at higher-tier cards instead.
faqTemplateAnswers.What are the best alternatives?: SBI Card Unnati or AU Spont Credit Card for a similar low-fee entry-level card segment.
